title: Constructors
description: The `new` operator and setting up instances.

questions.explanation: new creates {}, sets its prototype, binds `this` inside the function, and returns it.
Without new, `this` isn't the new object. Function returns undefined.
PascalCase signals a constructor — call with new.
Each new call returns a DIFFERENT instance.
Methods on prototype are shared by all instances.
Defensive constructor: if called without new, redirect.

questions.code:
function User() { this.x = 1; return { x: 42 }; }
console.log(new User().x);
questions.explanation: If a constructor explicitly returns an object, that overrides the new instance.

questions.code:
function User() { this.x = 1; return 42; }
console.log(new User().x);
questions.explanation: Returning a primitive is ignored; `this` is returned.
